intro: Tanacetum maresii, also known as Mares' chamomile, is a plant species in the daisy family, Asteraceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and Western China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities, making it useful in the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum maresii include flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, as well as sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tanacetum maresii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, colds, and flu.
